title: Confiture common JSON-Schema definitions
description: Shared sub-schemas referenced by per-command output schemas in this directory. Stable contract: changes require a top-level version bump in each consuming schema.

HintsArray.description: Advisory hints emitted on quiet-success states (e.g. zero migration files found, empty tracking table). Always present. Empty list when no hints are applicable. Hints never change the exit code — agents may treat presence of items as 'this success looks unusual'.

Violation.description: A single non-idempotent SQL pattern detected in a migration file.

pattern.description: Stable pattern id (see migrate-validate-list-patterns.schema.json for the full enumeration).

line_number.description: Line number where the violation starts. For .py migrations this is the line within the embedded SQL; see `source_line` for the .py file line.

source_line.description: Line number in the source file (.py file or .sql file). Only present when different from `line_number` — i.e. SQL embedded in a Python migration. NOTE: field name is `source_line`, NOT `line_number`.

suggestion.description: Suggested fix template. Placeholders may be filled with captured identifiers (table name, constraint name, etc.) when available.

fix_available.description: True when `confiture migrate fix --idempotent` can rewrite this violation automatically.

severity.description: `error` fails the gate (exit 1). `info` is a heuristic finding (e.g. CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW shape-risk) that's rendered but does NOT fail the gate unless `--strict-cor` is passed.

ExtractorWarning.description: A dynamic-SQL call the validator could not statically reach (e.g. f-string SQL, computed identifiers). Recorded so the user knows idempotency cannot be guaranteed for these snippets.

DriftItem.description: A single schema-drift finding between live database and expected DDL.
